name: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines

colors.symbolism: the tropical sky and the crystal waters of the sea
the warm Caribbean sunshine, the golden sands of the Grenadines and the spirit of the people
the lush vegetation and the enduring vitality of the people

description: Three vertical stripes in 1:2:1 proportions — blue, gold (double-width), green — with three green diamonds arranged in a V-shape centred on the gold band.
history: Adopted on 21 October 1985, the design replaced an earlier 1979 independence flag that bore a breadfruit leaf within a white centre disc. The current flag was designed by the Swiss graphic artist Julien van der Wal. The diamonds — sometimes called 'the Gems' — both spell V for Vincent and reference the country's traditional sobriquet, the 'Gems of the Antilles'.

features.symbolism: blue for the tropical sky and crystal-clear sea, gold for the warm sunshine and the friendliness of the people, and green for the lush vegetation of the islands

features.role: three diamonds in V-formation (Saint Vincent gems)
features.symbolism: the diamonds are arranged in the shape of a V for Vincent and recall the country's nickname 'the Gems of the Antilles'; the three figures together represent Saint Vincent and the Grenadines as the gems of the Antilles
